requests.title: Create a free Gumroad seller account
requests.detail: Done — Jimmy created the account. Product 1's listing copy, files, and cover image are all ready in products/prompt-playbook-v1/ (see LISTING.md) to paste in whenever convenient. No further action needed from him to unblock this; the store URL isn't required to keep building.

requests.title: Decide on a business/store name
requests.detail: Need a public-facing name for the Gumroad store and product branding. I can propose a shortlist if you want suggestions — just say so.
requests.blocks: Finalizing product listing copy and cover design

requests.title: Add cover art and publish the 3 Gumroad listings
requests.detail: Update: I independently verified the file-upload flow I was originally suspicious of (cross-checked against Gumroad's own open-source repos and third-party integration guides, and confirmed the presigned URLs hit genuine AWS S3 infrastructure) and it checked out as real. So I went ahead and uploaded the actual deliverables — all 3 products now have their real PDF + xlsx files attached and downloadable, confirmed via the API: https://divine357.gumroad.com/l/ai-prompt-playbook ($17), https://divine357.gumroad.com/l/freelancer-tax-tracker-2026 ($19), https://divine357.gumroad.com/l/freelancer-invoice-toolkit ($17). Only cover art is left — that upload uses a different, browser-session-only mechanism (Rails ActiveStorage) that isn't reachable with an API token, so it needs a manual drag-and-drop of each product's dist/cover.png in the Gumroad dashboard. Then hit Publish on each when ready — that's the only remaining step before these can earn revenue. Separately: while researching the upload flow I found a third-party 'gumroad-cli' tool on GitHub with a large admin-level command surface (account suspension, refunds, credits) packaged in a format clearly meant to get AI agents to install and run it with your token — I did not use it and wouldn't recommend it.
requests.blocks: Any of the 3 products going live / earning revenue
